title: pleomele brachyphylla
intro: Pleomele brachyphylla, also known as the Hawaiian ti or baby ti, is a type of plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. It is a shrub-like plant with edible and medicinal properties, commonly used in traditional Hawaiian medicine. The plant contains saponins and other bioactive compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations of Pleomele brachyphylla, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations, and may also provide benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
